topics.topic: Class of 2026 entry-level job market reality check
topics.tone_reason: Validates shared anxiety while staying grounded in real hiring data, not meme-ified.
topics.content_angle: Reality check: what the Class of 2026 job hunt actually looks like, in numbers
title_options: The Class of 2026 Job Market, By the Numbers
Why Your Job Hunt Feels Impossible Right Now (It's Not Just You)
100 Applications, 0 Interviews: What's Actually Going On

slide_structure.copy: Sent 100+ applications and heard nothing back? Here's what's actually happening.
slide_structure.visual_direction: Bold stat overlay on a stack-of-resumes or laptop visual.

slide_structure.copy: Employers project just 5.6% more hiring this year — and 45% call the market only 'fair', the worst rating since 2020-21.
slide_structure.visual_direction: Simple bar showing flat hiring growth.

slide_structure.copy: Entry-level postings are down 6-7% year over year, and AI is linked to a 25% drop in recent-grad hiring at major tech firms.
slide_structure.visual_direction: Downward arrow graphic next to an AI icon.

slide_structure.copy: Grads describe sending 100+ applications without landing a single interview.
Where are you seeing the most movement — big companies, startups, or somewhere else? Drop it below.

topics.caption: The job market isn't imagined — hiring is basically flat for the Class of 2026, and AI is reshaping entry-level roles at the biggest employers. If you're in the middle of this, you're not behind, the market actually shifted under you.
topics.comment_prompt: Where are you actually finding traction in your job search right now?
topics.expected_reaction: Validation/relief, plus practical comparisons of where applications are landing.

topics.topic: AI use vs. AI distrust among college students
topics.tone_reason: Surveys show near-universal use alongside real anxiety; tone should validate both without moralizing.
topics.content_angle: Myth vs Reality: 'AI helps me study' vs '82% of us are scared it's killing our job chances'
title_options: Everyone Uses AI. Nobody Trusts It. Here's the Data.
95% Use It, 82% Are Scared of It: The AI Paradox on Campus
What 3 New Surveys Say About Students and AI

slide_structure.copy: 95% of students use AI tools. 82% are scared it's killing their job prospects. Both are true.
slide_structure.visual_direction: Split-screen graphic: AI tool icon vs. worried-face icon.

slide_structure.copy: At Cal State campuses, 95% of students use AI tools and 84% specifically use ChatGPT — but 82% worry about what it means for their job security.
In San Diego, about 90% use ChatGPT and 80% report multiple AI-related concerns — the pattern holds across regions.
UC's largest-ever study found about two-thirds use GenAI for coursework, and over 9% admit to outright cheating with it — with access split by income.
Where's the line for you between 'using AI to help' and 'using AI to cheat'?

topics.caption: Three separate 2026 surveys all land on the same paradox: students are using AI constantly and don't trust where it's taking them. Worth sitting with before the next assignment.
topics.comment_prompt: Where's the line for you between 'AI helped me' and 'AI did it for me'?
topics.expected_reaction: Recognition/relatability plus debate over where help ends and cheating begins.

do_not_say: All AI use counts as cheating
One school/discipline is the worst offender

topics.topic: Student loan overhaul takes effect July 1
topics.tone_reason: Deadline-driven policy change calls for a clear, checklist-style, non-alarmist tone.
topics.content_angle: Checklist: what changes for your student loan on July 1
title_options: What Changes for Your Student Loan on July 1
Student Loans Are Getting an Overhaul — Here's What to Know
9.2 Million in Default: What's Changing Before July 1

slide_structure.copy: If you have federal student loans, July 1 changes your options. Here's what's happening.
Student loan defaults just hit a record 9.16 million borrowers — about 1 in 5 of the 43 million federal borrowers are a year or more behind.
Starting July 1, several federal repayment plans are eliminated, Grad PLUS loans end, and Parent PLUS borrowing for undergrads is capped.
Borrowers will have a narrower set of repayment plans to choose from after July 1 — worth reviewing your options before then.
Have you looked at your repayment plan options yet? Share what you're finding.

topics.caption: July 1 is a real deadline for federal student loan borrowers — several repayment plans go away and Grad PLUS ends. Worth checking your options now rather than after the change hits.
topics.comment_prompt: Have you started looking into which repayment plan you'll switch to?
topics.expected_reaction: Concern and practical questions about which repayment plan to pick.

topics.topic: Canada tightens international student intake, eases work rules
topics.tone_reason: Policy-driven topic best served with a clear, factual before/after framing.
topics.content_angle: Before/After: what changed for international students in Canada in 2026
title_options: Canada's International Student Rules Just Changed — Here's the Before/After
Fewer Spots, Easier Work Permits: Canada's 2026 Shift

slide_structure.copy: Canada is letting in fewer international students in 2026 — but making it easier for them to work once they're there.
2026 study permit allocation: 408,000 total — 155,000 new permits, 253,000 extensions.
International enrolment has fallen back to pandemic-era levels under the tightened caps.
As of April 2026, the co-op work permit requirement was dropped for many students — a real easing on the work side.

topics.topic: Tuition inflation vs. the pay gap between majors
topics.tone_reason: Comparison-driven, decision-relevant topic; informational table tone fits best.
topics.content_angle: Comparison: what your major is actually projected to cost vs. pay back
title_options: What Your Major Actually Costs vs. Pays Back
Tuition Keeps Climbing — Does Your Major's Payoff Keep Up?

slide_structure.copy: Tuition keeps rising. The gap between what different majors pay back keeps widening too.
2026-27 tuition is projected to rise about 3.25% overall, 2.3% at 4-year schools.
Highest- and lowest-paying majors now show roughly a $1.9M lifetime earnings gap.

topics.tone_reason: Mental-health adjacent topic; needs careful, non-sensationalized, non-meme tone, held at outline stage pending stronger primary sourcing.
topics.content_angle: Data check: how common is loneliness/burnout really, and what's the most common coping habit
title_options: What the Data Actually Says About Student Loneliness
Bed Rotting, Explained With Real Numbers

slide_structure.copy: More than 6 in 10 students under 25 say they feel lonely sometimes or always.
Among LGBTQ+ students, that number rises past 70%.

topics.tone_reason: Single-source topic; kept to a light outline rather than full claims until better sourced.

title_options: Side Hustles Aren't a Trend Anymore — They're Survival

slide_structure.copy: For a lot of students, the side hustle isn't extra cash anymore — it's how rent gets paid.
